codeReview.summary: Core schema changes (users/contacts/interactions columns, constraints, and indexes) are implemented, but the feature introduces a blocking runtime regression: contact creation paths accept payloads without userId while the DB now enforces contacts.user_id NOT NULL.

issues.description: createContact force-casts optional userId (`userId: insertContact.userId as string`) instead of guaranteeing a value. When userId is omitted, inserts hit a DB NOT NULL violation on contacts.user_id at runtime.

POST /api/contacts parses with insertContactSchema (where userId is optional in shared/schema.ts) and forwards directly to storage.createContact without attaching a fallback/derived userId. This path, and similar createContact callsites (e.g. webhook auto-create at routes.ts:681), now fails against contacts.user_id NOT NULL.

VAL-SCHEMA-005 test checks `SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 1`, which does not actually assert the seed user exists (username/placeholder fields). This can pass even if the specific seed behavior regresses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission docs do not currently codify a transitional convention for assigning contacts.user_id while legacy unauthenticated contact-creation routes are still active. This gap allowed a NOT NULL schema rollout that breaks existing create flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: shared/schema.ts:53 + shared/schema.ts:66-68 make DB user_id required but input optional; server/routes.ts:279-282 and 681-685 call storage.createContact without guaranteed userId.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A key schema rollout nuance (db:push + NOT NULL user_id with legacy create flows) is not captured in .factory/library. This increases risk of repeated regressions in future schema/auth work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 69b9ac8b-828d-4ff4-9569-f7dbdc7aaaac shows iterative recovery around user_id nullability/backfill, while .factory/library/architecture.md and environment.md do not document this migration hazard.

summary: Schema/table/index implementation is largely correct, but the feature should not be accepted as-is because contact creation can now fail at runtime when userId is omitted. Blocking remediation is needed to guarantee user_id assignment on create paths (or make schema/input constraints consistent) before marking complete.
